title: Cat or Kitten Vomiting Undigested Food: Triage and Care Guide
meta_desc: Is your kitten vomiting undigested food? Learn why this happens, when it is an emergency, and how to triage your cat's symptoms at home.
content_html:
What Is It When a Cat Vomits Undigested Food?

Seeing your cat or kitten bring up a meal that looks exactly like it did in the bowl can be alarming. This process is often technically 'regurgitation' rather than vomiting. It occurs when the food is expelled from the esophagus or stomach shortly after being swallowed, before the digestion process has truly begun. This can be caused by eating too quickly, food sensitivities, or hairballs.

Is It an Emergency?

The urgency level for a cat vomiting undigested food is generally Medium. While a single episode in an otherwise happy, active cat may just be a case of 'scarf and barf,' it becomes a concern if it happens repeatedly. You should contact a veterinarian immediately if the vomiting is accompanied by lethargy, a refusal to drink water, or if your kitten seems weak, as young cats can become dehydrated very rapidly.

Why Taking a Photo Helps Triage

It may feel strange, but taking a clear photo of the vomit can be incredibly helpful for your veterinary team. A photo allows the vet to see the color, volume, and texture of the material. It helps them distinguish between simple regurgitation and true vomiting, and can even reveal hidden clues like pieces of string, plastic, or blood that might not be obvious to the untrained eye. This visual evidence helps us determine how quickly your pet needs to be seen.

faqs.question: Why does my kitten vomit immediately after eating?
faqs.answer: The most common reason is eating too fast, which causes the stomach to expand too quickly and trigger a reflex to expel the food. This is often called 'scarf and barf.'

faqs.question: When should I be worried about my cat's vomiting?
faqs.answer: Seek veterinary care if your cat vomits more than twice in 24 hours, if they stop eating, or if they appear lethargic and hide.

faqs.question: Can I give my cat human medicine for vomiting?
faqs.answer: No, never give your cat human medications without a vet's direct instruction, as many common over-the-counter drugs are toxic to felines.
